item.title: Mathematician Agents
item.description:
Mathematician Agents

A mathematician agent is an agent whose skills include formal proof construction and whose tools include proof assistants (Lean, Agda, Coq, Isabelle). Within the agential semioverse framework, a mathematician agent is a triple (Sigma, K, Pi) where:

  • Sigma is bounded internal state (working memory of proof context, goals, hypotheses)
  • K is a set of interaction operators (proof tactics, term construction, type-checking)
  • Pi is a policy selecting admissible operators given the current state and universe

The key structural insight: the proof checker neutralizes hallucination. Unlike natural-language generation where errors propagate silently, a proof assistant rejects invalid reasoning immediately. This makes the agent loop fundamentally different from open-ended text generation — every proposed step is verified or rejected by the tool.

item.title: Agential Semioverse
item.description:
Abstract

Let’s construct an Agential Semioverse: an interactive-semioverse enriched with agents, skills, and tools, where all operations are mediated by a disciplined skill calculus and interpreted as interaction operators on partial states.

The Agential Semioverse extends the Interactive Semioverse by adding:

  • a set of agent handles with profiles (roles, goals, policies, memory);
  • a set of tools as primitive, typed, partial operators on states;
  • a skill calculus that composes tools into parametric procedures;
  • an execution semantics for agent-skill evaluation;
  • policy and authorization as monotone restrictions on admissible operators;
  • a fusion principle identifying skills with fragmentwise identical semantics.

All new operators must be fragment-preserving and closure-compatible. The resulting structure admits a least fixed point extension and is initial among agential semioverse structures over the same primitives.
